The classification of Ucriana wallichii places it in the order Gentianales and in the clade Asterids. It has been classified as a member of the family Rubiaceae, which is a large and diverse group of flowering plants. Ucriana wallichii is closely related to other genera within the Rubiaceae family, such as Psychotria and Urophyllum.

intro: Ucriana wallichii, also known as Saponaria officinalis, is a perennial plant species belonging to the Caryophyllaceae family, commonly found in the Mediterranean region. The plant is valued for its potential health benefits, including anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and antiseptic properties, which are said to aid in the treatment of respiratory issues, skin conditions, and wounds. The bioactive constituents of Ucriana wallichii include flavonoids, saponins, and phenolic acids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. Traditionally, the plant is used to prepare herbal teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are often used to treat respiratory issues, coughs, and sore throats.
